Title: Cousins
Sys Prompt: Respond only with the answer to the trolley problem - either pull the lever or don't, give brief explanation after
Prompt: Oh no! A trolley is heading towards one of your first cousins. You can pull the lever to divert it to the other track, killing 3 of your second cousins instead. What do you do?
opus_4_7_reasoning:
Don't pull the lever.
Genetically, you share roughly 12.5% of your genes with a first cousin and 3.125% with a second cousin. Three second cousins ≈ 9.375%, which is less than one first cousin. By Hamilton's rule (kin selection math), you'd save more "shared genes" by letting the trolley continue.
Morally, of course, this is a horrifying way to think about your relatives — but the puzzle as posed is really a kin-selection riddle dressed up as a trolley problem.
